qa_pairs.question: When running a multinode job, how can I copy individual files to the `SLURM_TMPDIR` directory on every allocated node?
qa_pairs.answer: To copy one or more files to the `SLURM_TMPDIR` directory on every node allocated for a multinode job, use the command: `srun --ntasks=$SLURM_NNODES --ntasks-per-node=1 cp file [files...] $SLURM_TMPDIR`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I extract a compressed archive, such as a ZIP file or tarball, into the `SLURM_TMPDIR` on every node in a multinode job?
qa_pairs.answer: To extract a ZIP archive to the `SLURM_TMPDIR` on every allocated node in a multinode job, use the command: `srun --ntasks=$SLURM_NNODES --ntasks-per-node=1 unzip archive.zip -d $SLURM_TMPDIR`. For a tarball, use `srun --ntasks=$SLURM_NNODES --ntasks-per-node=1 tar -xvf archive.tar.gz -C $SLURM_TMPDIR`.
